verdict_reason: Delta adds three handlers; the new catch-all 500 handler is untested (Major) and downgrades framework client errors like 405 to 500 (Minor); prior item 11 (Map.of vs ProblemDetail) still applies, prior item 12 (missing catch-all) is now resolved

todos.issue: New catch-all handleException (added since last review) has no test — GlobalExceptionHandlerTest covers handleUnreadableBody/handleNoResource/handleGatewayException and EventsSendControllerTest covers 400/415/502, but no test asserts an unexpected RuntimeException returns 500 with the {"error": "An unexpected error occurred"} body
todos.fix: Add a MockMvc test in EventsSendControllerTest (doThrow(new RuntimeException(...)) on queueMessageSender, expect status().isInternalServerError() + jsonPath $.error) or a direct unit test in GlobalExceptionHandlerTest

todos.issue: Catch-all @ExceptionHandler(Exception.class) also swallows framework client-error exceptions not handled above — e.g. GET /events throws HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException, which now returns 500 + error-level log instead of 405 (same class of problem the NoResourceFoundException handler was added to fix)
todos.fix: Handle Spring's ErrorResponse exceptions by honouring their own status (e.g. an @ExceptionHandler(ErrorResponseException.class)/HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException handler returning ex.getStatusCode()), or extend ResponseEntityExceptionHandler so framework exceptions keep their proper status codes
